original_abstract: This paper reviews the recent progress in the analysis of mobile phone datasets, a field that emerged a decade ago with the increasing availability of large-scale anonymized data. What began as a niche interest has since evolved into a robust, multidisciplinary area of research. We survey key contributions across several domains, including the structure of social networks, patterns of personal mobility, geographical partitioning, and urban planning. Furthermore, we examine the application of these datasets to international development, while addressing the critical challenges surrounding information security and individual privacy.
humanized_abstract: Analysis of Mobile Phone Data: A Survey of Techniques and Applications over the last decade, mobile phone data analysis has evolved from a research of local interest to a global field of study. In this survey paper, we give an overview of some works done in the analysis of mobile phone data in various fields, including social networks, human mobility patterns, clustering and geography and urban planning, as well as their contributions to international development and the associated data anonymization and privacy issues.
